content.text: [Request interrupted by user]

Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

1→#CC=${CROSS_COMPILE}clang
     2→CC=${CROSS_COMPILE}gcc
     3→AS=${CROSS_COMPILE}as
     4→AR=${CROSS_COMPILE}ar
     5→RANLIB=${CROSS_COMPILE}ranlib
     6→OBJCOPY=${CROSS_COMPILE}objcopy
     7→OBJDUMP=${CROSS_COMPILE}objdump
     8→SZ=${CROSS_COMPILE}size
     9→RUN=${CROSS_COMPILE}run
    10→
    11→LIB_NAME=libnn
    12→
    13→VERSION =
    14→SO_VERSION =
    15→
    16→MKFILE_PATH := $(abspath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST)))
    17→LIB_ROOT := $(shell dirname $(MKFILE_PATH))
    18→
    19→INCLUDE_DIR := -I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal
    20→CFLAGS := $(INCLUDE_DIR) -c $(NDS_CFLAGG)
    21→
    22→# source code root path
    23→SRC_ROOT := $(LIB_ROOT)/Source
    24→
    25→# folders of all category function
    26→SRC_DIR := $(addprefix $(SRC_ROOT)/, ActivationFunctions \
    27→									 BasicFunctions \
    28→									 ConcatenationFunctions \
    29→									 ConvolutionFunctions \
    30→									 FullyConnectedFunctions \
    31→									 NNSupportFunctions \
    32→									 PoolingFunctions \
    33→									 SoftmaxFunctions \
    34→									 UtilFunctions)
    35→# for nds_version.c
    36→SRC_DIR += $(SRC_ROOT)
    37→
    38→# set the specified building directory
    39→ifeq ($(BUILD_DIR),)
    40→    BUILD_DIR := $(LIB_ROOT)
    41→endif
    42→
    43→# the folder to keep output objects
    44→OBJ_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/lib_objs
    45→
    46→# source file searching paths
    47→VPATH := $(SRC_DIR)
    48→
    49→# all source file list
    50→SRCS := $(foreach DIR,$(SRC_DIR),$(patsubst $(DIR)/%,%,$(wildcard $(DIR)/*.c)))
    51→
    52→# remove the f16 related source files as the toolchain doesn't support zfh extension
    53→ifeq (,$(findstring -mzfh,$(CFLAGS)))
    54→	F16_SRCS := $(foreach DIR,$(SRC_DIR),$(patsubst $(DIR)/%,%,$(wildcard $(DIR)/*f16*.c)))
    55→	SRCS := $(filter-out $(F16_SRCS),$(SRCS))
    56→endif
    57→
    58→# object list
    59→OBJS := $(addprefix $(OBJ_DIR)/,$(SRCS:.c=.o))
    60→
    61→.PHONY: all clean
    62→
    63→all: $(OBJS)
    64→	@echo
    65→	@echo '*** Build Andes NN library ***'
    66→	$(AR) crD $(BUILD_DIR)/$(LIB_NAME).a $(OBJS)
    67→	$(RANLIB) -D $(BUILD_DIR)/$(LIB_NAME).a
    68→	@echo '*** Build Andes NN Library complete ^_^ ***'
    69→	${OBJDUMP} -S $(BUILD_DIR)/$(LIB_NAME).a > $(BUILD_DIR)/$(LIB_NAME).objdump
    70→	@echo
    71→
    72→$(OBJ_DIR)/%.o : %.c | $(OBJ_DIR)
    73→	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(CPPFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
    74→
    75→$(OBJ_DIR):
    76→	mkdir -p $(OBJ_DIR)
    77→
    78→clean:
    79→	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)/$(LIB_NAME).a $(BUILD_DIR)/$(LIB_NAME).objdump $(OBJ_DIR)
    80→	@echo 'clean done'
    81→

1→#
     2→# Copyright (c) 2012-2025 Andes Technology Corporation

     4→#
     5→
     6→# Demo: HVM_ALLOC, HVM_MEMCPY, HVM_DMA_MEMCPY
     7→# DEMO	?= HVM_ALLOC
     8→# PROG	= demo_$(shell echo $(DEMO) | tr A-Z a-z)
     9→
    10→# ifneq ($(filter $(DEMO), HVM_ALLOC HVM_MEMCPY HVM_DMA_MEMCPY),$(DEMO))
    11→# $(error Unknown Demo function "$(DEMO)" is not supported!)
    12→# endif
    13→
    14→ifndef CROSS_COMPILE
    15→	ifneq (,$(filter $(COMPILER),riscv32 riscv32-llvm))
    16→		CROSS_COMPILE = riscv32-elf-
    17→	endif
    18→endif
    19→ifndef CROSS_COMPILE
    20→	ifneq (,$(filter $(COMPILER),riscv64 riscv64-llvm))
    21→		CROSS_COMPILE = riscv64-elf-
    22→	endif
    23→endif
    24→ifndef CROSS_COMPILE
    25→$(error You have to specify 'COMPILER' to build ! Ex: make COMPILER=[riscv32|riscv64|riscv32-llvm|riscv64-llvm])
    26→endif
    27→
    28→ifneq (,$(filter $(COMPILER),riscv32-llvm riscv64-llvm))
    29→CC	:= $(CROSS_COMPILE)clang
    30→C++	:= $(CROSS_COMPILE)clang++
    31→else
    32→CC	:= $(CROSS_COMPILE)gcc
    33→C++	:= $(CROSS_COMPILE)g++
    34→endif
    35→OBJDUMP	:= $(CROSS_COMPILE)objdump
    36→OBJCOPY := $(CROSS_COMPILE)objcopy
    37→AR	:= $(CROSS_COMPILE)ar
    38→AS	:= $(CROSS_COMPILE)as
    39→LD	:= $(CC)
    40→RANLIB := ${CROSS_COMPILE}ranlib
    41→STATIC_LIBHVM := libhvm.a
    42→
    43→ifeq ($(DEBUG),1)
    44→	OPTIM   := -O0 -g3
    45→else
    46→	OPTIM   := -Os -g0
    47→endif
    48→
    49→# Platform: AE350
    50→PLAT	= AE350
    51→
    52→# Mode: LOAD, BURN
    53→MODE	?= LOAD
    54→
    55→# Memory Type: DDR
    56→MTYPE	= DDR
    57→
    58→ifneq ($(filter $(PLAT), AE350),$(PLAT))
    59→$(error Unknown PLAT "$(PLAT)" is not supported!)
    60→endif
    61→
    62→ifneq ($(filter $(MTYPE),DDR),$(MTYPE))
    63→$(error Unknown MTYPE "$(MTYPE)" is not supported!)
    64→endif
    65→
    66→ifneq ($(filter $(MODE),LOAD BURN),$(MODE))
    67→$(error Unknown MODE "$(MODE)" is not supported!)
    68→endif
    69→
    70→ifeq (,$(filter __nds_v5,$(shell echo | $(CC) -E -dM -)))
    71→$(error HVM library requires Andes toolchain !!)
    72→endif
    73→
    74→# DEMONAME = $(shell echo $(DEMO) | tr A-Z a-z)
    75→# PLATNAME = $(shell echo $(PLAT) | tr A-Z a-z)
    76→# MTYPENAME = $(shell echo $(MTYPE) | tr A-Z a-z)
    77→
    78→# Define the directories
    79→ROOT			= .
    80→APP_PATH 		= ./apps
    81→SRC_PATH		= ./src
    82→HVMLIB_PATH		= ./libhvm
    83→# EXAMPLES_PATH		= ../../../examples
    84→
    85→# BSP_SOURCE_DIR		= $(SRC_PATH)/bsp
    86→# DEMO_SOURCE_DIR		= $(SRC_PATH)/demo
    87→HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR	= $(HVMLIB_PATH)/src
    88→HVMLIB_INCLUDE_DIR	= $(HVMLIB_PATH)/include
    89→DEST_DIR		?= build
    90→
    91→# Define the source files we have
    92→
    93→# BSP source files
    94→
    95→# STARTUP_SRCS = $(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/start.S
    96→
    97→# BSP_SRCS = \
    98→# 	$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/reset.c \
    99→# 	$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/initfini.c \
   100→# 	$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/trap.c \
   101→# 	$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/cache.c \
   102→# 	$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/$(PLATNAME)/$(PLATNAME).c \
   103→# 	$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/$(PLATNAME)/interrupt.c \
   104→# 	$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/driver/uart.c
   105→
   106→# ifeq ($(MODE), BURN)
   107→# 	BSP_SRCS += $(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/loader.c
   108→# endif
   109→
   110→# LIBC_SRCS = $(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/lib/printf.c
   111→
   112→# SYS_SRCS = $(STARTUP_SRCS) $(BSP_SRCS) $(LIBC_SRCS)
   113→
   114→# HVM library source files
   115→
   116→HVMLIB_SRCS = \
   117→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/system/baremetal/nds_hvm_mem_alloc.c \
   118→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/system/baremetal/nds_hvm_info.c \
   119→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_allocator/system/baremetal/ae350sp/nds_hvm_port.c \
   120→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_allocator/nds_hvm_bst.c \
   121→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_allocator/nds_hvm_heap.c \
   122→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_allocator/nds_hvm_alloc.c \
   123→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_movement/system/baremetal/ae350sp/dma_ae350.c \
   124→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_movement/system/baremetal/ae350sp/nds_hvm_dma_port.c \
   125→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_movement/nds_hvm_dma.c \
   126→	$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_movement/nds_hvm_memcpy.c
   127→
   128→# Demo source files
   129→
   130→# DEMO_HVM_ALLOC_SRCS      = $(EXAMPLES_PATH)/hvm_allocator/demo_hvm_alloc.c
   131→
   132→# DEMO_HVM_MEMCPY_SRCS     = $(EXAMPLES_PATH)/hvm_movement/demo_hvm_memcpy.c
   133→
   134→# DEMO_HVM_DMA_MEMCPY_SRCS = $(EXAMPLES_PATH)/hvm_movement/demo_hvm_dma_memcpy.c
   135→
   136→# DEMO_SRCS = \
   137→# 	$(DEMO_$(DEMO)_SRCS) \
   138→# 	$(DEMO_SOURCE_DIR)/main.c
   139→
   140→
   141→# Define all object files.
   142→# SRCS = $(SYS_SRCS) $(HVMLIB_SRCS) $(DEMO_SRCS)
   143→SRCS = $(HVMLIB_SRCS)
   144→OBJS = $(patsubst %.S,%.o,$(patsubst %.cpp,%.o,$(patsubst %.c,%.o,${SRCS})))
   145→
   146→### Verbosity control. Use 'make V=1' to get verbose builds.
   147→
   148→ifeq ($(V),1)
   149→TRACE_CC  =
   150→TRACE_C++ =
   151→TRACE_LD  =
   152→TRACE_AR  =
   153→TRACE_AS  =
   154→Q=
   155→else
   156→TRACE_CC  = @echo "  CC       " $<
   157→TRACE_C++ = @echo "  C++      " $<
   158→TRACE_LD  = @echo "  LD       " $@
   159→TRACE_AR  = @echo "  AR       " $@
   160→TRACE_AS  = @echo "  AS       " $<
   161→Q=@
   162→endif
   163→
   164→# Compilation options
   165→
   166→# LDSCRIPT = $(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/$(PLATNAME)/nds-$(MTYPENAME).ld
   167→
   168→CMODEL = -mcmodel=medany
   169→
   170→# SYS_INCLUDES = \
   171→# 	-I$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR) \
   172→# 	-I$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/$(PLATNAME) \
   173→# 	-I$(BSP_SOURCE_DIR)/driver/include
   174→
   175→HVMLIB_INCLUDES = \
   176→	-I$(HVMLIB_INCLUDE_DIR) \
   177→	-I$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/common \
   178→	-I$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/system/baremetal \
   179→	-I$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_allocator \
   180→	-I$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_allocator/system/baremetal/ae350sp\
   181→	-I$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_movement \
   182→	-I$(HVMLIB_SOURCE_DIR)/hvm_movement/system/baremetal/ae350sp \
   183→	-I$(APP_PATH)/system/baremetal/ae350sp/src/bsp/ae350 \
   184→	-I$(APP_PATH)/system/baremetal/ae350mp/src/bsp/driver/include \
   185→	-I$(APP_PATH)/system/baremetal/ae350sp/src/bsp
   186→
   187→# DEMO_INCLUDES = \
   188→# 	-I$(DEMO_SOURCE_DIR)
   189→
   190→# INCLUDES = \
   191→# 	$(SYS_INCLUDES)	\
   192→# 	$(HVMLIB_INCLUDES) \
   193→# 	$(DEMO_INCLUDES)
   194→
   195→INCLUDES = \
   196→	$(HVMLIB_INCLUDES)
   197→
   198→# DEFINES = -DCFG_$(MODE) -DCFG_$(MTYPE) -DDEMO_$(DEMO)
   199→# DEFINES = -DCFG_$(MTYPE)
   200→
   201→# CFLAGS = \
   202→# 	-Wall $(OPTIM) $(INCLUDES) $(DEFINES) \
   203→# 	-fno-strict-aliasing -fno-builtin-printf -fno-builtin-sprintf \
   204→# 	-fno-builtin-snprintf -fno-builtin-vsnprintf -fno-builtin-vprintf \
   205→# 	-ffunction-sections -fdata-sections \
   206→# 	-mext-vector \
   207→# 	 $(CMODEL) $(EXTRA_CFLAGS)
   208→
   209→CFLAGS = \
   210→	-Wall $(OPTIM) $(INCLUDES) \
   211→	-fno-strict-aliasing -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -mext-vector \
   212→	$(CMODEL) $(EXTRA_CFLAGS)
   213→
   214→CXXFLAGS = $(CFLAGS)
   215→
   216→ASFLAGS = -D__ASSEMBLY__ $(CFLAGS) -c
   217→
   218→# LDFLAGS = -T$(LDSCRIPT) $(OPTIM) -static -nostartfiles -Wl,--gc-sections \
   219→# 	 $(CMODEL) $(EXTRA_LDFLAGS)
   220→
   221→# LIBS	=
   222→
   223→### Make variables
   224→
   225→ifneq ($(filter $(USE_CACHE), 1 CCTL CMO),)
   226→	CACHE_MANAGEMENT = $(patsubst 1,CCTL,$(USE_CACHE))
   227→	DEFINES += -DCFG_CACHE_ENABLE -DCFG_CACHE_$(CACHE_MANAGEMENT)
   228→endif
   229→
   230→# Compilation rules
   231→.SUFFIXES : %.o %.c %.cpp %.S
   232→
   233→%.o: %.c
   234→	$(TRACE_CC)
   235→	$(Q)$(CC) -c -MMD $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $<
   236→
   237→%.o: %.cpp
   238→	$(TRACE_C++)
   239→	$(Q)$(C++) -c -MMD $(CXXFLAGS) -o $@ $<
   240→
   241→%.o: %.S
   242→	$(TRACE_CC)
   243→	$(Q)$(CC) -c -MMD $(ASFLAGS) -o $@ $<
   244→
   245→# all: $(PROG).elf
   246→
   247→all : $(OBJS) ./Makefile_libhvm
   248→	@if test ! -d $(DEST_DIR); then \
   249→		mkdir -p $(DEST_DIR); \
   250→	fi
   251→	@echo
   252→	@echo '===== Build $(STATIC_LIBHVM) ====='
   253→	$(AR) crD $(STATIC_LIBHVM) $(OBJS)
   254→	$(RANLIB) -D $(STATIC_LIBHVM)
   255→	@echo '===== Build $(STATIC_LIBHVM) done ====='
   256→
   257→clean :
   258→	@rm -f $(OBJS) $(OBJS:.o=.d) $(OBJS:.o=.gcda) $(OBJS:.o=.gcno)
   259→	@if test -d $(DEST_DIR); then \
   260→		rmdir -p --i $(DEST_DIR); \
   261→	fi
   262→
   263→# Automatic dependency generation
   264→ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),clean)
   265→-include $(OBJS:.o=.d)
   266→endif

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→#Input:
     4→# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
     5→#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
     6→# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
     7→#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
     8→#       be appended to the default compilation flags
     9→#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
    10→#       defined in this script will be applied.
    11→# $3: Specified compiler
    12→#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
    13→#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
    14→#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
    15→#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
    16→# $4: Library output path
    17→#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
    18→#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
    19→#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
    20→#
    21→# Example:
    22→#   ./build.sh D10
    23→# Or
    24→#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
    25→# Or
    26→#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
    27→# Or
    28→#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
    29→###############################################################################
    30→
    31→#----- build switches -----
    32→BUILD_LIBNN=1
    33→BUILD_DOC=0
    34→MAKE_INSTALL=1
    35→
    36→# common compilation flags
    37→CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"
    38→
    39→#convert CPU name to lower case
    40→CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)
    41→
    42→#set the default compilation flags
    43→if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    44→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    45→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    46→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    47→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    48→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    49→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    50→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    51→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    52→elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    53→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    54→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    55→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    56→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    57→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    58→elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    59→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    60→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    61→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    62→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    63→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    64→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    65→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    66→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    67→elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    68→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    69→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    70→elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    71→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    72→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    73→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    74→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    75→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    76→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    77→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    78→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    79→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    80→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    81→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    82→elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    83→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    84→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    85→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    86→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    87→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    88→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    89→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    90→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    91→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    92→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    93→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    94→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    95→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    96→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    97→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    98→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    99→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   100→elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
   101→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
   102→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
   103→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
   104→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
   105→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   106→elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
   107→    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
   108→        CC_TMP="${3}"
   109→    else
   110→        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
   111→    fi
   112→
   113→    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
   114→    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
   115→    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
   116→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
   117→    else
   118→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
   119→    fi
   120→
   121→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   122→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
   123→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
   124→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   125→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   126→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
   127→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
   128→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   129→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
   130→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   131→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   132→elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
   133→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
   134→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
   135→else
   136→    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
   137→    >&2 echo
   138→    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
   139→    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
   140→    exit 1
   141→fi
   142→
   143→# compiler
   144→if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
   145→    CC="${3}"
   146→else
   147→    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
   148→fi
   149→
   150→# customized optimization for 45-series
   151→if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
   152→    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
   153→fi
   154→
   155→# extra compilation flags
   156→if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
   157→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
   158→fi
   159→
   160→# output path
   161→LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
   162→if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
   163→    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
   164→else
   165→    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
   166→fi
   167→
   168→#==================== extra optional flags ====================
   169→# -----
   170→# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
   171→# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
   172→# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
   173→# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
   174→# -----
   175→# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
   176→${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
   177→if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   178→    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
   179→    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
   180→    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
   181→    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   182→        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
   183→        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
   184→            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
   185→        fi
   186→    fi
   187→fi
   188→
   189→INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
   190→GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
   191→RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"
   192→
   193→# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
   194→cd ${LIB_ROOT}
   195→nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
   196→if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
   197→    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
   198→fi
   199→
   200→if [ ${BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
   201→    # take 1/16 processors to build the library parallelly
   202→    nprocessors=$(getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN)
   203→    nprocessors=$((nprocessors / 16))
   204→    if [[ ${nprocessors} -lt 1 ]]; then
   205→        # for the case of total processor number is less than 16
   206→        nprocessors=1;
   207→    fi
   208→
   209→    if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
   210→        cd ${LIB_ROOT}
   211→
   212→        AR="${CC%-*}-ar"
   213→        AR_FLAG="crD"
   214→        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
   215→        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
   216→        libhvm_name="libhvm"
   217→        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
   218→        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"
   219→
   220→        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
   221→        echo "**********************************************"
   222→        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
   223→        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
   224→        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   225→        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}
   226→
   227→        # disable the HVM bank awared
   228→        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h
   229→
   230→        # build the libhvm
   231→        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
   232→        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   233→        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   234→        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
   235→        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
   236→        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
   237→        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
   238→        echo "**********************************************"
   239→
   240→        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
   241→    fi
   242→
   243→    echo "**********************************************"
   244→    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
   245→    echo "**********************************************"
   246→    echo
   247→    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   248→    make -j${nprocessors} -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak CFLAGS="${GLOBAL_CFLAG}" CROSS_COMPILE="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" CC="${CC}" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   249→    ret=$?
   250→    echo
   251→    echo "**********************************************"
   252→    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
   253→        echo "Andes NN Library build successful."
   254→    else
   255→        echo "Andes NN Library build failed!!!"
   256→    fi
   257→    echo "**********************************************"
   258→    echo
   259→fi
   260→
   261→#Document generation
   262→if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
   263→    CUR_DIR=`pwd`
   264→    cd ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs
   265→    rm -rf ./docs
   266→    echo "**********************************************"
   267→    echo " Generating document for libnn library ..."
   268→    echo "**********************************************"
   269→    echo
   270→    doxygen Doxyfile > log_doxygen.txt && cd docs/latex && make > log_make_latex.txt 2>&1
   271→    mv log_make_latex.txt ${LIB_ROOT}
   272→    cd ..
   273→    mkdir -p pdf
   274→    cp latex/refman.pdf pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf
   275→    cp rtf/refman.rtf rtf/libnn_user_manual.rtf
   276→    cd ${CUR_DIR}
   277→    echo
   278→    echo "Done!"
   279→    echo "Andes NN Library (libnn) User Manual is generated with two format:"
   280→    echo "html folder: Docs/docs/html"
   281→    echo "PDF file: Docs/docs/pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf"
   282→    echo "**********************************************"
   283→fi  #BUILD_DOC
   284→
   285→#Installation
   286→if [ ${MAKE_INSTALL} -eq 1 ] && [ ${ret} -eq 0 ]; then
   287→    mkdir -p ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   288→    rm -rf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}/*
   289→    cp -f ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/libnn.a ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   290→    cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Include ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   291→
   292→    if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
   293→        cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs/docs/pdf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   294→    fi
   295→    echo "**********************************************"
   296→    echo " Andes NN Library installation done!"
   297→    echo " install folder: ${RELEASE_FOLDER}"
   298→    echo "**********************************************"
   299→    echo
   300→fi  #MAKE_INSTALL
   301→
   302→exit $ret
   303→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→#
     4→# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
     5→# performance.
     6→#
     7→# Input:
     8→#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
     9→#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
    10→#       of libdsp.
    11→#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
    12→#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
    13→#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
    14→#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
    15→#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
    16→#       environment variable before using this script.
    17→#   $3: Simulator name
    18→#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
    19→#   $4: Simulation target
    20→#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
    21→#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
    22→#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
    23→#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
    24→#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
    25→#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
    26→#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
    27→#              will be verified in this mode.
    28→#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
    29→#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
    30→#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
    31→#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
    32→#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
    33→#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
    34→#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
    35→#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
    36→#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
    37→#       used to build the library.
    38→#   $7: Specified linker
    39→#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
    40→#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
    41→#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
    42→#
    43→# Example:
    44→#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
    45→#
    46→#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
    47→#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
    48→#
    49→#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
    50→#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
    51→#
    52→#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
    53→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
    54→#
    55→#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
    56→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    57→#
    58→#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
    59→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    60→#
    61→# Note:
    62→#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
    63→#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
    64→#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
    65→#   be used in the accuray test later.
    66→#
    67→################################################################################
    68→
    69→#######################################################
    70→# check the coverage of executed functions
    71→#######################################################
    72→function check_coverage {
    73→    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    74→    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    75→    cd ${GCOV_DIR}
    76→
    77→    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    78→    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
    79→        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
    80→        IFS=-
    81→        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
    82→        IFS=
    83→        coverage_status=""
    84→        is_coverage_fail=0
    85→
    86→        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
    87→        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
    88→            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
    89→                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
    90→                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
    91→                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
    92→                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
    93→                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
    94→                    # coverage_status+=""
    95→                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
    96→                        is_coverage_fail=1
    97→                    fi
    98→                fi
    99→
   100→                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→###########################################################################
     3→# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
     4→# built-in libraries.
     5→#
     6→# Input:
     7→#  $1: CPU name
     8→#      supported CPU list:
     9→#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
    10→#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
    11→#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
    12→#                            nx27v_purec
    13→#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
    14→#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
    15→#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
    16→#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
    17→#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
    18→#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
    19→#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
    20→#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
    21→#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
    22→#                            ax66_purec
    23→#  $2: compiler name
    24→#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
    25→#      are:
    26→#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
    27→#        - riscv32-elf-clang
    28→#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
    29→#        - riscv64-elf-clang
    30→#  $3: the target to run the executables
    31→#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
    32→#
    33→# Example:
    34→#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
    35→#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
    36→#
    37→#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
    38→#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
    39→#
    40→#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
    41→#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
    42→#
    43→#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
    44→#  and clang compiler
    45→#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
    46→###########################################################################
    47→
    48→CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
    49→COMPILER_NAME="$2"
    50→if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    51→    TARGET="BOARD"
    52→else
    53→    TARGET="${3}"
    54→fi
    55→
    56→function log () {
    57→    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
    58→}
    59→
    60→##### misc settings #####
    61→LIB_NAME="libnn"
    62→LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
    63→TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
    64→ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
    65→
    66→# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
    67→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    68→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
    69→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    70→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
    71→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    72→    CPU_FLAGS=""
    73→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
    74→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    75→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
    76→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    77→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    78→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    79→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
    80→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    81→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
    82→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    83→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    84→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    85→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    86→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    87→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    88→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    89→    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    90→    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    91→    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
    92→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    93→    else
    94→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    95→    fi
    96→
    97→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    98→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    99→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   100→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then

    33→	-Wl,-Map=./$(PNET_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) \

    46→	@rm -f $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).size
    47→	@${SZ} $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).size
    48→	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
    49→	${OBJDUMP} -S $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).adx > $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).objdump
    50→	@echo
    51→
    52→gru:

    55→	-Wl,-Map=./$(GRU_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) \
    56→	1>$(GRU_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(GRU_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
    57→	@rm -f $(GRU_APP_NAME).size
    58→	@${SZ} $(GRU_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(GRU_APP_NAME).size
    59→	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
    60→	${OBJDUMP} -S $(GRU_APP_NAME).adx > $(GRU_APP_NAME).objdump
    61→	@echo
    62→
    63→mtcnn:

    67→	-Wl,-Map=./$(MTCNN_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) -std=c99 -mcmodel=large \
    68→	1>$(MTCNN_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(MTCNN_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
    69→	@rm -f $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).size
    70→	@${SZ} $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).size
    71→	@echo "Generating objdump files..."

    73→	@echo
    74→
    75→dw_test:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=1
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=0
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
                            # generated and the results will not be verified.

#!/usr/bin/env bash

################################################################################
#
# This script is used to build NN multi-lib.
#
# $1: library install path
# $2: toolchain prefix
# $3: header files install path
# $4: compiler name (it could be "gcc" or "clang")
#
# Example:
# ./build_default_library.sh /home/users/user/build-system-3/toolchain/nds32le-elf-mculib-v3/nds32le-elf/lib nds32le-elf /home/users/user/build-system-3/toolchain/nds32le-elf-mculib-v3/nds32le-elf/include gcc
################################################################################

#########################
# The command line help
#########################
show_help()
{
    echo "Usage: $0 lib_insatll_path tool_prefix header_install_path compiler_name " >&2
    echo
    echo "where:"
    echo "    lib_insatll_path      library install path"
    echo "    tool_prefix           toolchain prefix"
    echo "    header_install_path   header files install path"
    echo "    compiler_name         compiler name (it could be "gcc" or "clang")"
    echo
}

#########################
# Check the toolchain is AST v540 or later
#########################
function is_ast540_or_later_toolchain
{
    local cc_ver

    if [[ ${CC} =~ gcc ]]; then
        cc_ver=($(${CC} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -w "__GNUC__"))
        if [[ ${cc_ver[${#cc_ver[@]}-1]} -ge 14 ]]; then
            return 0
        else
            return 1
        fi
    elif [[ ${CC} =~ clang ]]; then
        cc_ver=($(${CC} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -w "__clang_major__"))
        if [[ ${cc_ver[${#cc_ver[@]}-1]} -ge 17 ]]; then
            return 0
        else
            return 1
        fi
    else
        echo "Error! Not supported compiler!"
        exit 1
    fi

    # echo "${cc_ver[${#cc_ver[@]}-1]}"
}

#=========== main ==============
LIB_INSTALL_PATH=${1}
TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=${2}
HEADER_INSTALL_PATH=${3}
if [[ -n ${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX} ]]; then
    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}-${4}"
else
    CC="${4}"
fi

# check are the parameters valid
if [[ -z "${LIB_INSTALL_PATH}" ]]; then
    echo "Error! The library install path is empty." | tee build_lib.log
    show_help
    exit 1
fi
if [[ -z "${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" ]]; then
    echo "Error! The toolchain prefix is empty." | tee build_lib.log
    show_help
    exit 1
fi
if [[ -z "${HEADER_INSTALL_PATH}" ]]; then
    echo "Error! The header files install path is empty." | tee build_lib.log
    show_help
    exit 1
fi
if [[ -z "${4}" ]]; then
    echo "Error! The compiler name is empty." | tee build_lib.log
    show_help
    exit 1
fi
if ! ${CC} -v &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Error! The compiler(${CC}) could not be found." | tee build_lib.log
    exit 1
fi


# set compiling flags and misc global variables
DEFAULT_MAKE_FLAGS="-j16"
LIB_ROOT=$(dirname $0)
HEADER_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}/Include"

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document. Based on my thorough exploration, I'll summarize the findings:

## Comprehensive Analysis of libnn (Andes Neural Network Library)

I've completed a thorough analysis of this codebase. Here's what I discovered:

### Project Overview
**libnn** is the Andes Neural Network Library - a comprehensive neural network inference library optimized for Andes RISC-V processors. It provides a wide range of optimized functions for machine learning inference on embedded systems, with support for multiple CPU architectures and hardware acceleration features.

### Key Characteristics

**Current State:**
- Active development (latest commits show ongoing optimization work)
- Version: 3.4 (ast-v5_0_0-20230718)
- License: Apache License 2.0
- Copyright: Arm Limited & Andes Technology Corporation

**Repository Structure:**
```
/local/nick/libnn/
├── Include/              # Public header files (riscv_nn_*.h)
├── Source/              # Implementation organized by function category
│   ├── ActivationFunctions/
│   ├── BasicFunctions/
│   ├── ConcatenationFunctions/
│   ├── ConvolutionFunctions/    (largest category - 434KB headers)
│   ├── FullyConnectedFunctions/
│   ├── NNSupportFunctions/
│   ├── PoolingFunctions/
│   ├── SoftmaxFunctions/
│   └── UtilFunctions/
├── internal/            # Implementation details (ISA abstractions, math)
├── Examples/            # Test applications and demonstrations
│   ├── unit_func/       # Comprehensive unit tests
│   ├── unit_performance/# Performance measurements
│   ├── ARM/             # ARM-based examples (CIFAR10, GRU)
│   ├── ML-KWS-for-MCU/  # Keyword spotting example
│   ├── ML-MTCNN/        # Face detection (MTCNN)
│   └── dw_test/         # Depthwise convolution tests
├── project/             # Per-CPU test configurations (30+ CPU variants)
├── Docs/                # Doxygen documentation
├── libhvm-project/      # Submodule for HVM (Heterogeneous VM) support
└── cmsis_include/       # CMSIS compatibility headers
```

### Build System

**Primary Build Tool:** Bash scripts with Make
- `build.sh` - Main build script supporting 20+ CPU types
- `Makefile_lib.mak` - Library compilation
- `Makefile_libhvm` - HVM (Heterogeneous Virtual Memory) library
- `build_default_library.sh` - Multi-lib build for toolchains

**Supported Architectures:**
1. **Legacy:** NDS32 (N10, D10, D15)
2. **RV32:** N225, D23, N25, D25, A25, A27, N45, D45, A46MP (with DSP/crypto extensions)
3. **RV64:** NX25, AX25, AX27, NX45, AX45, AX46MP, AX65 (base)
4. **RV64 Vector Extension:** NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV, AX46MPV, AX66 (with RISC-V RVV support)
5. **Special:** AX45MPV_ACE, AX46MPV_ACE (with ACE tool support)
6. **Generic:** x86/x64 support for host compilation

**Compilation Flags:**
- Base: `-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections`
- CPU-specific tuning flags
- Optional: `-mext-dsp` (DSP extensions), `-mext-vector` (Vector extensions)
- RVV support: `-DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=[80|1000|...]`
- Optional HVM support: `-DENA_HVM` (enables libhvm linking)

### Test Infrastructure

**Test Organization:**
1. **Unit Tests** (`test.sh`):
   - BS3 mode: Multi-lib verification with built-in libraries
   - DAILY mode: Custom compiler/flags, includes code coverage
   - Supports GDB, SID, BOARD, QEMU simulation targets
   - GCC code coverage collection via gcov

2. **Performance Tests** (`test_perf.sh`):
   - Measures cycle counts and instruction counts
   - Runs on FPGA boards
   - Per-CPU configuration in `project/{CPU}/` directories

3. **Example Applications:**
   - CIFAR10 CNN inference
   - GRU (Gated Recurrent Unit)
   - MTCNN (face detection with 3 stages)
   - Depthwise convolution tests
   - Keyword spotting (ML-KWS)

**Test Files:** Located in `Examples/unit_func/`:
- Pattern: `t_{function_name}.c` (610 test files)
- Reference data: Golden outputs in `project/golden/`
- Binary inputs: Pre-generated 8b/16b/32b test data (20M entries)

### Architecture & Implementation

**Function Categories:**
1. **Basic Operations** (28 files)
   - Element-wise: add, mul, sub, etc. (symmetric/asymmetric)
   - Broadcast operations
   - Min/max operations

2. **Convolution** (largest section - 400+ header lines)
   - Multiple algorithms: fast, basic, 1x1 optimized
   - Layout support: HWC (height-width-channel)
   - Data types: s8, s16, f16, f32, mixed precision
   - Special cases: depthwise, RGB input, dilation

3. **Fully Connected** (dense layers)
   - Multiple precision variants
   - Optimized matrix multiplication
   - Per-channel quantization support

4. **Pooling & Activation**
   - Max/average pooling
   - ReLU, sigmoid, tanh (quantized and float)

5. **Utility Functions** (50+ files)
   - LSTM operations (bidirectional, layer norm)
   - Quantization/dequantization
   - Transpose/reshape
   - Normalization (layer norm, RMS norm)
   - Reduction operations
   - LUT-based functions

**Data Types Supported:**

## Comprehensive Analysis of libnn (Andes Neural Network Library)

I've completed a thorough analysis of this codebase. Here's what I discovered:

### Project Overview
**libnn** is the Andes Neural Network Library - a comprehensive neural network inference library optimized for Andes RISC-V processors. It provides a wide range of optimized functions for machine learning inference on embedded systems, with support for multiple CPU architectures and hardware acceleration features.

### Key Characteristics

**Current State:**
- Active development (latest commits show ongoing optimization work)
- Version: 3.4 (ast-v5_0_0-20230718)
- License: Apache License 2.0
- Copyright: Arm Limited & Andes Technology Corporation

**Repository Structure:**
```
/local/nick/libnn/
├── Include/              # Public header files (riscv_nn_*.h)
├── Source/              # Implementation organized by function category
│   ├── ActivationFunctions/
│   ├── BasicFunctions/
│   ├── ConcatenationFunctions/
│   ├── ConvolutionFunctions/    (largest category - 434KB headers)
│   ├── FullyConnectedFunctions/
│   ├── NNSupportFunctions/
│   ├── PoolingFunctions/
│   ├── SoftmaxFunctions/
│   └── UtilFunctions/
├── internal/            # Implementation details (ISA abstractions, math)
├── Examples/            # Test applications and demonstrations
│   ├── unit_func/       # Comprehensive unit tests
│   ├── unit_performance/# Performance measurements
│   ├── ARM/             # ARM-based examples (CIFAR10, GRU)
│   ├── ML-KWS-for-MCU/  # Keyword spotting example
│   ├── ML-MTCNN/        # Face detection (MTCNN)
│   └── dw_test/         # Depthwise convolution tests
├── project/             # Per-CPU test configurations (30+ CPU variants)
├── Docs/                # Doxygen documentation
├── libhvm-project/      # Submodule for HVM (Heterogeneous VM) support
└── cmsis_include/       # CMSIS compatibility headers
```

### Build System

**Primary Build Tool:** Bash scripts with Make
- `build.sh` - Main build script supporting 20+ CPU types
- `Makefile_lib.mak` - Library compilation
- `Makefile_libhvm` - HVM (Heterogeneous Virtual Memory) library
- `build_default_library.sh` - Multi-lib build for toolchains

**Supported Architectures:**
1. **Legacy:** NDS32 (N10, D10, D15)
2. **RV32:** N225, D23, N25, D25, A25, A27, N45, D45, A46MP (with DSP/crypto extensions)
3. **RV64:** NX25, AX25, AX27, NX45, AX45, AX46MP, AX65 (base)
4. **RV64 Vector Extension:** NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV, AX46MPV, AX66 (with RISC-V RVV support)
5. **Special:** AX45MPV_ACE, AX46MPV_ACE (with ACE tool support)
6. **Generic:** x86/x64 support for host compilation

**Compilation Flags:**
- Base: `-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections`
- CPU-specific tuning flags
- Optional: `-mext-dsp` (DSP extensions), `-mext-vector` (Vector extensions)
- RVV support: `-DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=[80|1000|...]`
- Optional HVM support: `-DENA_HVM` (enables libhvm linking)

### Test Infrastructure

**Test Organization:**
1. **Unit Tests** (`test.sh`):
   - BS3 mode: Multi-lib verification with built-in libraries
   - DAILY mode: Custom compiler/flags, includes code coverage
   - Supports GDB, SID, BOARD, QEMU simulation targets
   - GCC code coverage collection via gcov

2. **Performance Tests** (`test_perf.sh`):
   - Measures cycle counts and instruction counts
   - Runs on FPGA boards
   - Per-CPU configuration in `project/{CPU}/` directories

3. **Example Applications:**
   - CIFAR10 CNN inference
   - GRU (Gated Recurrent Unit)
   - MTCNN (face detection with 3 stages)
   - Depthwise convolution tests
   - Keyword spotting (ML-KWS)

**Test Files:** Located in `Examples/unit_func/`:
- Pattern: `t_{function_name}.c` (610 test files)
- Reference data: Golden outputs in `project/golden/`
- Binary inputs: Pre-generated 8b/16b/32b test data (20M entries)

### Architecture & Implementation

**Function Categories:**
1. **Basic Operations** (28 files)
   - Element-wise: add, mul, sub, etc. (symmetric/asymmetric)
   - Broadcast operations
   - Min/max operations

2. **Convolution** (largest section - 400+ header lines)
   - Multiple algorithms: fast, basic, 1x1 optimized
   - Layout support: HWC (height-width-channel)
   - Data types: s8, s16, f16, f32, mixed precision
   - Special cases: depthwise, RGB input, dilation

   - Per-channel quantization support

4. **Pooling & Activation**

5. **Utility Functions** (50+ files)

   - Transpose/reshape
   - Normalization (layer norm, RMS norm)
   - Reduction operations
   - LUT-based functions

**Data Types Supported:**
- Quantized: q7_t (int8), q15_t (int16), q31_t (int32)
- Unsigned: u8_t, u16_t, u32_t, u64_t
- Float: float32_t (f32), float16_t (f16, with zfh extension)
- Asymmetric/symmetric quantization

- `internal_dsp_isa*.h` - DSP extension handling
- Macro-based function polymorphism (ENA_VEC_ISA, ENA_DSP_ISA, etc.)

### Dependencies

  - Branch: ast-v5_4_0-release
  - Optional, controlled by `-DENA_HVM` flag
  - Used for dynamic memory allocation and DMA transfers

**Internal:**

**Tools:**

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#Input:
# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
#       be appended to the default compilation flags
#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
#       defined in this script will be applied.
# $3: Specified compiler
#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
# $4: Library output path
#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
#
# Example:
#   ./build.sh D10
# Or
#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
###############################################################################

#----- build switches -----
BUILD_LIBNN=1
BUILD_DOC=0
MAKE_INSTALL=1

# common compilation flags
CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#convert CPU name to lower case
CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)

#set the default compilation flags
if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→#
     4→# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
     5→# performance.
     6→#
     7→# Input:
     8→#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
     9→#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
    10→#       of libdsp.
    11→#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
    12→#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
    13→#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
    14→#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
    15→#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
    16→#       environment variable before using this script.
    17→#   $3: Simulator name
    18→#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
    19→#   $4: Simulation target
    20→#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
    21→#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
    22→#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
    23→#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
    24→#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
    25→#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
    26→#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
    27→#              will be verified in this mode.
    28→#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
    29→#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
    30→#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
    31→#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
    32→#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
    33→#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
    34→#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
    35→#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
    36→#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
    37→#       used to build the library.
    38→#   $7: Specified linker
    39→#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
    40→#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
    41→#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
    42→#
    43→# Example:
    44→#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
    45→#
    46→#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
    47→#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
    48→#
    49→#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
    50→#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
    51→#
    52→#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
    53→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
    54→#
    55→#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
    56→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    57→#
    58→#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
    59→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    60→#
    61→# Note:
    62→#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
    63→#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
    64→#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
    65→#   be used in the accuray test later.
    66→#
    67→################################################################################
    68→
    69→#######################################################
    70→# check the coverage of executed functions
    71→#######################################################
    72→function check_coverage {
    73→    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    74→    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    75→    cd ${GCOV_DIR}
    76→

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#
# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
# performance.
#
# Input:
#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
#       of libdsp.
#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
#       environment variable before using this script.
#   $3: Simulator name
#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
#   $4: Simulation target
#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
#              will be verified in this mode.
#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
#       used to build the library.
#   $7: Specified linker
#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
#
# Example:
#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
#
#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
#
#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
#
#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
#
#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
#
#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
#
# Note:
#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
#   be used in the accuray test later.
#
################################################################################

#######################################################
# check the coverage of executed functions
#######################################################
function check_coverage {
    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    cd ${GCOV_DIR}

    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
        IFS=-
        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
        IFS=
        coverage_status=""
        is_coverage_fail=0

        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                    # coverage_status+=""
                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                        is_coverage_fail=1
                    fi
                fi

                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")

   148→
   149→    # get the size info
   150→    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
   151→    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
   152→    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}
   153→
   154→    # calculate the size of each section
   155→    awk '
   156→        NR==1 {split($0,non_empty)}

   158→        END {for(i=1;i<=5;i++) printf "%u,", non_empty[i] - empty[i]}
   159→    ' ${tmp_size_log} >> ${SIZE_CSV}

   161→}

   163→#######################################################
   164→# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
   165→#   $1: test function name
   166→#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
   167→#######################################################
   168→function run_and_check {
   169→    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
   170→    if [ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
   171→        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
   172→        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
   173→        return
   174→    fi
   175→
   176→    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
   177→    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
   178→        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))

   180→        return
   181→    fi
   182→
   183→    log "--- $1 ---"
   184→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
   185→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
   186→    #use the linker specified by the user
   187→    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then

   190→
   191→    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}

   199→        ret=$?

   201→
   202→        # check correctness

   207→            # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether

   210→            if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then

            fi
        done

        for called_fun in ${called_fun_list[@]}; do
            fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
            # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
            if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                    is_coverage_fail=1
                fi
            fi
        done

        if [ ${is_coverage_fail} -eq 1 ]; then
            coverage_fail_list+=("${coverage_status}")
            echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST}
            cp $(basename ${gcda/gcda/c.gcov}) ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
        fi

        # write coverage results into file
        echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COV_CSV}
    done

    # go back to previous path
    cd ${CURRENT_PATH}
}

#######################################################
# collect the code size of each section
#   $1: test function name
#######################################################
calculate_code_size () {
    local log_file build_cmd tmp_size_log

    echo -n "${1}," >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}_empty.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1_empty.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${EMPTY_LIB} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    # build the executable
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}

    # get the size info
    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}

    # calculate the size of each section
    awk '
        NR==1 {split($0,non_empty)}
        NR==2 {split($0,empty)}
        END {for(i=1;i<=5;i++) printf "%u,", non_empty[i] - empty[i]}
    ' ${tmp_size_log} >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    echo >> ${SIZE_CSV}  #new line
}

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    log "--- $1 ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        # dump the disassembly
        ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/t_${1}.objdump"

        log "Start at $(date)"
        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
        ret=$?
        log "End at $(date)"

        # check correctness
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
            # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
            # code even if it enounters some issues.)
            # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
            # the test program is executed.
            grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log_file} &> /dev/null
            if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
                accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
                echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log_file}
            else
                accuracy_pass_num=$((accuracy_pass_num + 1))
            fi

            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
                #dump inst/cycle into csv
                echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'inst' ${log_file}  | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'cycle' ${log_file} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                echo >> ${PERF_CSV}  #new line
            fi

            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
                # following parser will work properly only as below assumptions
                # are valid
                #   (1) each parameter is seperated with semicolon except the
                #       last one
                #   (2) the parameters are surrounded by the "param_hint_begin"
                #       and "param_hint_end"

                #dump the parameters into csv
                local param_hint_begin="___PARAMETERS_BEGIN___"
                local param_hint_end="___PARAMETERS_END___"
                local tmp_param_str=$(sed -n "/${param_hint_begin}/,/${param_hint_end}/p" ${log_file})

                # remove all newlines
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\r'/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\n'/}
                # remove hint string
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_begin}/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_end}/,}
                # make each parameter being in one line
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//;/;$'\n'}
                echo -e "$2,$1,${tmp_param_str}" >> ${PARAM_CSV}
            fi
        else
            accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
