CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
        CC_TMP="${3}"
    else
        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
    fi

    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
else
    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
    >&2 echo
    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
    exit 1
fi

# compiler
if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
    CC="${3}"
else
    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
fi

# customized optimization for 45-series
if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
fi

# extra compilation flags
if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
fi

# output path
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
else
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
fi

#==================== extra optional flags ====================
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
# -----
# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)

#CC=${CROSS_COMPILE}clang
CC=${CROSS_COMPILE}gcc
AS=${CROSS_COMPILE}as
AR=${CROSS_COMPILE}ar
RANLIB=${CROSS_COMPILE}ranlib
OBJCOPY=${CROSS_COMPILE}objcopy
OBJDUMP=${CROSS_COMPILE}objdump
SZ=${CROSS_COMPILE}size
RUN=${CROSS_COMPILE}run

LIB_NAME=libnn

VERSION =
SO_VERSION =

MKFILE_PATH := $(abspath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST)))
LIB_ROOT := $(shell dirname $(MKFILE_PATH))

INCLUDE_DIR := -I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal
CFLAGS := $(INCLUDE_DIR) -c $(NDS_CFLAGG)

# source code root path
SRC_ROOT := $(LIB_ROOT)/Source

# folders of all category function
SRC_DIR := $(addprefix $(SRC_ROOT)/, ActivationFunctions \
									 BasicFunctions \
									 ConcatenationFunctions \
									 ConvolutionFunctions \
									 FullyConnectedFunctions \
									 NNSupportFunctions \
									 PoolingFunctions \
									 SoftmaxFunctions \
									 UtilFunctions)
# for nds_version.c
SRC_DIR += $(SRC_ROOT)

# set the specified building directory
ifeq ($(BUILD_DIR),)
    BUILD_DIR := $(LIB_ROOT)
endif

# the folder to keep output objects
OBJ_DIR := $(BUILD_DIR)/lib_objs

# source file searching paths
VPATH := $(SRC_DIR)

# all source file list
SRCS := $(foreach DIR,$(SRC_DIR),$(patsubst $(DIR)/%,%,$(wildcard $(DIR)/*.c)))

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

libnn is the Andes Neural Network Library — an optimized neural network inference library for Andes RISC-V processors. It provides quantized (s8/s16/s4) and floating-point (f16/f32) implementations of common NN operations (convolution, fully connected, pooling, activation, softmax, LSTM, etc.) with multiple optimization tiers: plain C, DSP extensions (`-mext-dsp`), packed extensions (RV32P/RV64P), and RISC-V Vector extension (RVV 0.8/1.0).

## Build Commands

**Build the library** for a specific CPU:
```bash
./build.sh <CPU>                              # e.g., ./build.sh D25
./build.sh <CPU> "<extra_flags>"              # e.g., ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops"
./build.sh <CPU> "" <compiler>                # e.g., ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
```

Supported CPUs: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV, AX46MPV, x86, x64 (case insensitive).

Output: `libnn.a` in repository root (or specified output path via $4).

**Toolchain prefixes** (auto-selected by CPU):
- NDS32 (N10/D10/D15): `nds32le-elf-`
- RV32 (N225/D23/D25/A25/A27/...): `riscv32-elf-`
- RV64 (NX25/AX25/AX27/NX45/AX45/NX27V/...): `riscv64-elf-`
- x86/x64: no prefix (host GCC)

**Common CFLAGS**: `-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections`

## Running Tests

Tests must be run from a separate build directory:
```bash
mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir

# Plain C algorithm with GDB simulator
../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3

# DSP algorithm with RV32 SID simulator
../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"

# V-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID simulator
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"

# DAILY mode (builds library + runs accuracy + code coverage)
../test.sh "" <compiler> <simulator> <target> DAILY "<flags>"
```

test.sh arguments: `$1`=source path (dummy), `$2`=compiler, `$3`=simulator, `$4`=target (GDB/SID/BOARD/QEMU), `$5`=BS3 or DAILY, `$6`=extra flags, `$7`=linker.

Unit test sources: `Examples/unit_func/t_<function_name>.c` (~610 tests).

## Code Architecture

### Source Organization

Each function category lives under `Source/<Category>/`:
- **ActivationFunctions** — ReLU, sigmoid, tanh (quantized variants)
- **BasicFunctions** — element-wise add/mul/sub, broadcast ops
- **ConvolutionFunctions** — conv2d (basic/fast/1x1), depthwise, dilation; HWC layout
- **FullyConnectedFunctions** — dense layers, per-channel quantization
- **PoolingFunctions** — max/average pooling
- **SoftmaxFunctions** — softmax variants
- **ConcatenationFunctions** — tensor concatenation
- **NNSupportFunctions** — mat-mul kernels, data conversion helpers
- **UtilFunctions** — LSTM (uni/bidirectional, layer-norm), quantize/dequantize, transpose, reshape, reduction, RMS norm

### Multi-tier Implementation Pattern

Most functions have multiple implementations selected at compile time via preprocessor macros:
1. **Plain C** — baseline, always available
2. **DSP** (`ENA_DSP_ISA`) — uses Andes DSP/packed extensions (`-mext-dsp`)
3. **Vector** (`ENA_VEC_ISA`) — uses RISC-V V-extension (`-mext-vector`)

Within a single `.c` file, you'll see `#if defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) ... #elif defined(ENA_DSP_ISA) ... #else ... #endif` blocks.

### Internal Headers (`internal/`)

- `internal_nn_math.h` — core math primitives, SIMD intrinsics wrappers
- `internal_vec_*.h` — vector ISA abstraction layers (multiple files for RVV 0.8, 0.9, 1.0)
- `internal_dsp_isa*.h` — DSP extension helpers
- `internal_nds_config.h` — feature toggle macros

### Public API (`Include/`)

Headers organized by category: `riscv_nn_convolution.h`, `riscv_nn_basic.h`, etc. Types in `riscv_nn_types.h` and `riscv_math_types.h`. All public functions are prefixed `riscv_nn_`.

### Key Preprocessor Defines

- `ENA_VEC_ISA` — enable vector extension code paths
- `NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION` — RVV version (80=v0.8, 1000=v1.0)
- `MAX_VLEN` — maximum vector register length in bits
- `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` — enable Andes V5 vector dot product
- `ENA_DSP_ISA` — auto-defined when `-mext-dsp` is used
- `ENA_HVM` — enable heterogeneous VM support (links libhvm)
- `ENA_45_SERIES_OPT` — auto-added for 45-series CPUs
- `ENA_ACE_RVV` — enable ACE (Andes Custom Extension) with RVV

## Commit Message Convention

Commits use bracketed prefixes: `[opt/rv64p]`, `[opt/rv32p]`, `[opt/rvv]`, `[opt/dsp]`, `[bug-fix]`, `[fine-tune]`, `[misc]`, `[feature]`. Example: `[opt/rv64p] Optimize the algorithm in lstm_unidirectional_s16 with RV64P.`

## Data Types

- `q7_t` (int8), `q15_t` (int16), `q31_t` (int32) — quantized types
- `float16_t` (f16, requires `-mzfh`), `float32_t` (f32)
- Function suffixes indicate precision: `_s8`, `_s16`, `_s4`, `_f16`, `_f32`

Headers organized by category: `riscv_nn_convolution.h`, `riscv_nn_basic.h`, etc. Types in `riscv_nn_types.h` and `riscv_math_types.h`. All public functions are prefixed `riscv_nn_`.

### Key Preprocessor Defines

- `ENA_VEC_ISA` — enable vector extension code paths
- `NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION` — RVV version (80=v0.8, 1000=v1.0)
- `MAX_VLEN` — maximum vector register length in bits
- `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` — enable Andes V5 vector dot product
- `ENA_DSP_ISA` — auto-defined when `-mext-dsp` is used
- `ENA_HVM` — enable heterogeneous VM support (links libhvm)
- `ENA_45_SERIES_OPT` — auto-added for 45-series CPUs
- `ENA_ACE_RVV` — enable ACE (Andes Custom Extension) with RVV

## Commit Message Convention

Commits use bracketed prefixes: `[opt/rv64p]`, `[opt/rv32p]`, `[opt/rvv]`, `[opt/dsp]`, `[bug-fix]`, `[fine-tune]`, `[misc]`, `[feature]`. Example: `[opt/rv64p] Optimize the algorithm in lstm_unidirectional_s16 with RV64P.`

## Data Types

- `q7_t` (int8), `q15_t` (int16), `q31_t` (int32) — quantized types
- `float16_t` (f16, requires `-mzfh`), `float32_t` (f32)
- Function suffixes indicate precision: `_s8`, `_s16`, `_s4`, `_f16`, `_f32`

- **Build commands** — `build.sh` usage with CPU targets, toolchain prefixes, and common flags

- **Code architecture** — source organization by function category, multi-tier implementation pattern (plain C / DSP / Vector), internal header structure
- **Key preprocessor defines** — the macros that control code path selection
- **Commit convention** — bracketed prefix style used in this repo
- **Data types** — quantized and float type naming conventions

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-nx45
CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/ADP-AE350-NX45.cfg

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the QEMU exists
if [ -z ${QEMU} ]; then
    echo "Error! QEMU path is not set up yet."
    echo "Please export the QEMU path to an environment variable named \$QEMU_SYS_RV64 first."
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?

cd ${OLD_PWD}

exit ${ret}

    20→ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
    21→RUN_CONFIG=1        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
    22→ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
    23→ENA_LM=0            #0: place the data into the cacheable region, 1: place the data into the local memory
    24→ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
    25→
    26→#----- flags of library and app -----
    27→CPU_NAME="nx45"

    29→APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
    30→LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
    31→COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
    32→APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
    33→LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"
    34→
    35→# enable mvh and link to the customized linker script
    36→if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${ENA_LM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    37→    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh -Wl,-T ${CWD}/ae350-xip.ld -DENA_LM"

    42→if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then

    46→#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
    47→if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    48→    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx45"

    51→else
    52→    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-nx45-v5"
    53→fi
    54→
    55→if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    56→    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
    57→else
    58→    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
    59→fi
    60→
    61→#----- extra flags -----
    62→# -----
    63→# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
    64→# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
    65→# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
    66→# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
    67→# -----
    68→# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
    69→${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
    70→if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    71→    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    72→    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    73→    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    74→    if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
    75→        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"

    89→
    90→
    91→
    92→#----- app running configuration -----
    93→# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
    94→# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
    95→# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
    96→# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
    97→APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

    99→if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=1        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_LM=0            #0: place the data into the cacheable region, 1: place the data into the local memory
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="nx45"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh and link to the customized linker script
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${ENA_LM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh -Wl,-T ${CWD}/ae350-xip.ld -DENA_LM"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx45"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-nx45-v5"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [[ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ]];then
    cd ../../
    build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        exit $?
    fi
    cd ${CWD}
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-nx27v,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

content.text:
Here's how toolchain and QEMU are set up in this project:

## Toolchain Setup

The toolchain is configured via `NLIB_CROSS*` files and environment `PATH`:

1. **Add the Andes toolchain to your `PATH`:**
   ```bash
   export PATH=/path/to/andes-toolchain/bin:$PATH
   ```

2. **Toolchain prefix is auto-selected by CPU** in `build.sh`:
   - RV32 CPUs (N225, D23, D25, A25, A27, N45, D45, A46MP, A46MPV): `riscv32-elf-gcc`
   - RV64 CPUs (NX25, AX25, AX27, NX45, AX45, AX46MP, NX27V, AX45MPV, AX46MPV, AX65, AX66): `riscv64-elf-gcc`
   - NDS32 CPUs (N10, D10, D15): `nds32le-elf-gcc`

3. **Override compiler** via the 3rd argument to `build.sh`:
   ```bash
   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc      # use linux toolchain
   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang      # use clang
   ```

4. **Per-project config files** (e.g. `project/NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64`) define the full tool set:
   ```
   CROSS_COMPILE=riscv64-elf
   CC=${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc
   AR=${CROSS_COMPILE}-ar
   OBJDUMP=${CROSS_COMPILE}-objdump
   ...
   ```

## QEMU Setup

1. **Export the QEMU binary path** as an environment variable:
   ```bash
   # For RV64 CPUs:
   export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/path/to/qemu-system-riscv64
   ```
   The QEMU wrappers check this variable and error out if it's not set.

2. **Each CPU has its own QEMU wrapper** under `project/<cpu>/`:
   ```
   project/nx45/riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx45
   project/nx27v/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_s512_b512   # different VLEN configs
   project/n25/riscv-qemu-wrapper-n25
   ...
   ```

3. **The wrappers invoke QEMU** with CPU-specific configuration:
   ```bash
   # What the wrapper does internally:
   $QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE
   ```
   - `-M andes_ae350` — Andes AE350 machine model
   - `-cpu andes-nx45` (or `andes-nx27v,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512` for vector CPUs)
   - `-readconfig` points to a `.cfg` file in the project directory
   - `-semihosting` enables host I/O for test output

4. **Using QEMU with `run.sh`** — set `RUN_CONFIG=2` in the per-project `run.sh`:
   ```bash
   # In project/nx45/run.sh:
   RUN_CONFIG=2    # 0=BOARD, 1=SID, 2=QEMU
   ```

5. **Using QEMU with `test.sh`** — pass `QEMU` as the test target:
   ```bash
   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx45 QEMU BS3
   ```

6. **For vector CPUs (NX27V)**, QEMU wrappers support different VLEN configurations and optional `VEC_CFG` (zve32x, zve32f, zve64x, zve64f, zve64d):
   ```bash
   export VEC_CFG=zve64d   # optional, defaults to zve64d
   ```

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input:
     4→#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
     5→################################################################################
     6→CWD=$(pwd)
     7→
     8→#----- set up the tool name -----
     9→source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64
    10→
    11→#----- build and run switches -----
    12→ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
    13→ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
    14→ENA_RUN_GRU=0
    15→ENA_RUN_PNET=0
    16→ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
    17→ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
    18→ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
    19→ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
    20→ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
    21→RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
    22→ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
    23→ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
    24→ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
    25→ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
    26→ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
    27→ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
    28→ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
    29→ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
    30→ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
    31→ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
    32→VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d
    33→
    34→#----- flags of library and app -----
    35→CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
    36→CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    37→APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
    38→LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
    39→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    40→    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
    41→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    42→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    43→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    44→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    45→        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    46→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
    47→        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    48→    else
    49→        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    50→    fi
    51→
    52→    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    53→    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    54→    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
    55→fi
    56→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    57→    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
    58→fi
    59→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    60→    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
    61→fi
    62→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    63→    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    64→    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
    65→fi
    66→COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
    67→if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    68→    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    69→    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    70→    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    71→fi
    72→APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
    73→LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"
    74→
    75→# enable mvh
    76→if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    77→    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
    78→fi
    79→
    80→if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    81→    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
    82→fi
    83→if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    84→    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    85→    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    86→fi
    87→if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    88→    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
    89→fi
    90→
    91→if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    92→    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
    93→fi
    94→
    95→#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
    96→if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    97→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    98→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    99→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
   100→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
   101→    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
   102→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
   103→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
   104→elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
   105→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
   106→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
   107→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
   108→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
   109→    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
   110→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
   111→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
   112→else
   113→    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
   114→fi
   115→
   116→if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
   117→    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
   118→else
   119→    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
   120→fi
   121→
   122→#----- extra flags -----
   123→# -----
   124→# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
   125→# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
   126→# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
   127→# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
   128→# -----
   129→# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
   130→${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
   131→if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

content.text:
The `run.sh` already has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU) and uses `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` by default. I need to:

1. Change the QEMU wrapper to the VLEN 1024 variant (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`)
2. Set `QEMU_SYS_RV64` to point to the user's QEMU binary
3. Enable all tests

Let me check the QEMU wrapper for VLEN 1024 to confirm it uses `vlen=1024`.

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

lines: RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler

    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

content.text:
As expected — this is upstream GCC 14.1.0, not the Andes toolchain. The `-mext-vector` flag is Andes-specific.

**You need the Andes RISC-V toolchain** to build this library. The standard `riscv64-elf-gcc` from Arch repos doesn't support Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`, `-mext-dsp`, `-mtune=andes-45-series`, etc.

Do you have the Andes toolchain installed somewhere? If so, tell me the path and I'll add it to `PATH` and re-run. The toolchain binary would typically be named `riscv64-elf-gcc` but located in the Andes SDK directory (e.g., something like `/path/to/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5/bin/`).

#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

content.text:
I can see in `_write` at `0x19140-0x19144`: `slli zero,zero,0x1f` followed by `ebreak` — this is the **Andes Virtual Hosting** (VH) semihosting sequence. After the ebreak there's `srai zero,zero,0x7` which is the VH signature postfix.

The key is syscall number in `a0=5` (SYS_write). QEMU's `-semihosting` should handle this. Let me check if QEMU needs a specific VH-related option.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then

    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi

LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then

    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then

fi

fi

    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else

fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else

fi

#----- extra flags -----

# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with

    fi
fi

fi



#----- app running configuration -----

# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

content.text: No writes to fd 1, 2, or 3 at all. The semihosting handler is being triggered (we saw 12 ebreaks in the trace) but it's not producing any host write syscalls. Let me check the QEMU semihosting source to understand why.

The semihosting uses ARM-compat semihosting syscall numbers, but the Andes VH uses different syscall numbers! In the binary, `_write` sets `a0=5` which matches `TARGET_SYS_WRITE=0x05`. But let me check — the Andes VH might use a different calling convention.
OK so the flow is: ebreak detected → `RISCV_EXCP_SEMIHOST` → `do_common_semihosting(cs)`. Let me check the common-semi-target.h to understand the register mapping.

So `common_semi_arg(cs, 0)` reads `a0`, `common_semi_arg(cs, 1)` reads `a1`, etc. Looking at the binary's `_write` function:

```
a0 = 5 (SYS_WRITE)
a1 = sp (pointer to args struct)
```

Wait — `do_common_semihosting` reads `a0` as the syscall number and `a1` as the args pointer. But the binary sets `a0 = 5` before the semihosting call. Let me look more carefully at the VH protocol.

So `nr = a0 = 5` (SYS_WRITE), `args = a1 = sp` (pointer to the argument block on stack). Looking at the binary's `_write`:
```
a0 = 5           # SYS_WRITE
a1 = sp          # pointer to [fd_entry, buf_ptr, len] on stack
```

And `SYS_WRITE` case:
```c
case TARGET_SYS_WRITE:
    GET_ARG(0);  // fd handle from args[0]
    GET_ARG(1);  // buffer ptr from args[1]  
    GET_ARG(2);  // length from args[2]
    semihost_sys_write(cs, common_semi_rw_cb, arg0, arg1, arg2);
```

The issue might be that the guest fd (arg0) doesn't map to a valid host fd. The guest needs to have opened `:tt` (stdout) first via SYS_OPEN. Let me check if there's an initialization issue. Let me also try a simpler approach — run with `-d trace:semihosting*` to see semihosting calls.

content.text:
`<not executed on terminal>` — the script command shows it wasn't run on a terminal. The sandbox doesn't provide a TTY. With `-nographic`, QEMU tries to use the serial console via the monitor on stdio, but `-nographic` combined with no terminal might cause the semihosting chardev to fail silently.

Let me try a different approach — use `-serial none -monitor none` to prevent QEMU from trying to use stdio for the serial/monitor, and instead let semihosting use it directly.

content.text:
Now I can see the fdtable initialization at `0x12ab8`:
- `fdtable[0]` (stdin): first word = 0 (fd 0)
- `fdtable[1]` (stdout): first word = 1 (set at `0x12ac8-0x12aca`)
- `fdtable[2]` (stderr): first word = 2 (set at `0x12acc-0x12ace`)

So when `_write` is called for stdout, `__get_fdentry(1)` returns a struct whose first word is `1`. Then in the semihosting call, `a0=5` (SYS_WRITE), and the args struct has `[1, buf, len]`.

But QEMU's semihosting `SYS_WRITE` treats arg0 as a **guest fd handle** from its `guestfd` table, not a raw host fd! QEMU's guestfd 0/1/2 are only valid if the guest opened them via `SYS_OPEN(":tt", ...)`. Since the Andes newlib skips `SYS_OPEN` and uses raw host fd numbers directly, QEMU doesn't know about them and silently drops the writes.

This is the root cause. I need to either:
1. Modify QEMU to handle raw host fds, or
2. Build the tests without `-mvh` and use a different I/O method, or  
3. **Pre-open the guest fds** by patching the QEMU invocation

Actually, let me check if there's a QEMU option to auto-map console fds.

Found it! The key is `CONFIG_ARM_COMPATIBLE_SEMIHOSTING`:

- **With `CONFIG_ARM_COMPATIBLE_SEMIHOSTING`**: guestfd_array starts empty. Console in/out go to a separate `GuestFDConsole` type. Guest fd handles from `SYS_WRITE` must have been allocated via `SYS_OPEN`.
- **Without `CONFIG_ARM_COMPATIBLE_SEMIHOSTING`** (the `#else` at line 42): guestfd_array is pre-sized to 3 entries, with stdin(0)/stdout(1)/stderr(2) pre-mapped.

RISC-V in this QEMU build likely has `CONFIG_ARM_COMPATIBLE_SEMIHOSTING` defined (since it uses the ARM-compat semihosting path). So guest fd 1 (stdout) is **not pre-mapped**, and `SYS_WRITE` with fd=1 fails silently.

Now I have the complete picture. With `CONFIG_ARM_COMPATIBLE_SEMIHOSTING`, the guestfd_array starts **empty**. When `SYS_WRITE` is called with fd=1, `get_guestfd(1)` returns NULL because there's no entry in the array. The write is silently dropped.

The fix: patch `guestfd.c` to pre-populate the guestfd_array with stdin/stdout/stderr for the ARM-compatible semihosting path as well, since Andes VH doesn't use SYS_OPEN.

content.content: The file /local/nick/qemu_andes/semihosting/guestfd.c has been updated successfully.

lines: +     * Pre-map guest fds 0/1/2 (stdin/stdout/stderr) so that guests using
+     * Andes Virtual Hosting (VH) can use raw fd numbers in SYS_WRITE
/* Otherwise, the stdio file descriptors apply. */

content.text: The patch works (it's now hitting the guestfd entries) but there's a secondary issue — `SYS_FLEN` (get file length) is being called on a console fd, which asserts. The `GuestFDConsole` type doesn't support `SYS_FLEN`. The Andes newlib init likely calls `SYS_FLEN` on stdout to check if it's a regular file. Let me use `GuestFDHost` instead of `GuestFDConsole` for the pre-mapped fds.
The `GuestFDConsole` doesn't support `SYS_FLEN`. I should use `associate_guestfd` (which maps to `GuestFDHost` with real host fds) for all cases.
